Latest Article
What Really
Fails a GCC?
Analysis · LinkedIn Article

What Really Fails a GCC?

Most GCC failures are not about strategy, technology, or even talent. They are about the invisible things — governance gaps, misaligned expectations between the parent company and the India leadership, and the absence of a clear mandate. This article unpacks the patterns behind GCC underperformance.

SP
Shammi Prabhakar Singh
Founder, GCCPROs · CA & GCC Strategist
Read on LinkedIn →
GCC Insider Convergence · March 2026

8 Harsh Truths for the
Future of GCCs

Unfiltered insights from the GCC Insider Convergence — straight from the leaders running India's most influential GCCs. These are real voices, real perspectives, real accountability.

1 / 9
GCC Insider Convergence · Mar 2026
"We will never have a hub or a spoke model, we will have a hub AND spoke model — both will coexist."
Sandeep Poddar
Kimberly-Clark
💡 Why it matters

The Tier 1 vs. Tier 2 city debate is over. The future of location strategy isn't binary — it's about placing the right capabilities in the right ecosystems.

2 / 9
GCC Insider Convergence · Mar 2026
"In the next 5 years, technical will be a commodity. The judgment will be the premium."
Vinod Shah
Zendesk
💡 Why it matters

AI can generate half your code or automate your reconciliations. What it can't do is frame the complex business problem. System thinking is your new competitive moat.

3 / 9
GCC Insider Convergence · Mar 2026
"Time is not the risk. It's essentially the stagnation of skills that will be the risk."
Srinivas Sampath
Upland Software
💡 Why it matters

10 years of experience means nothing if it's just 1 year of experience repeated 10 times. Longevity only protects you if you are constantly reinventing your value.

4 / 9
GCC Insider Convergence · Mar 2026
"There is no reason why you can't do a CFO role sitting here in India."
Atul Chanani
Citi Bank
💡 Why it matters

The geographic ceiling has shattered. With GCCs at the center of global data flows, enterprise leadership roles are no longer restricted to the HQ.

5 / 9
GCC Insider Convergence · Mar 2026
"You have to start thinking of business outcomes versus just pure technical skills."
Ruchi Dubey
Metro
💡 Why it matters

GCCs are looking for Business Partnering skills — the ability to translate technical output into measurable impact for global stakeholders.

6 / 9
GCC Insider Convergence · Mar 2026
"I don't think you just need an upskilling of your skill set. I think you need right-skilling."
Sameer Shaikh
XPO
💡 Why it matters

Hyper-specialization in a dying process is a trap. You don't just need to learn more — you need to learn the right things that align with where your GCC is heading.

7 / 9
GCC Insider Convergence · Mar 2026
"Tell yourself that you can work on strategic finance. Don't wait for anybody to tell you that."
Nitin Madan
Barclays
💡 Why it matters

GCC leaders need to stop acting like execution arms waiting for a mandate. Take the initiative and claim your seat at the table before it's explicitly offered.

8 / 9
GCC Insider Convergence · Mar 2026
"One of the most important skills everybody has to learn is learn how to learn."
Jayesh Prajapati
SLB
💡 Why it matters

We are shifting from an IQ-driven era to an EQ and adaptability-driven era. The tools will keep changing — your superpower is how fast you can unlearn and relearn.

9 / 9
GCC Insider Convergence · Mar 2026
"The journey from specialist to generalist is where the real trust lies. Always add your perspective to whatever you do in a GCC."
Tarun Arora
Protiviti
💡 Why it matters

Being a silent executioner is a career killer. To break out of the back-office stereotype, speak up, share your strategic viewpoint, and ensure global stakeholders understand your impact.

Opinion · GCC Strategy

What Really Fails a GCC? The Invisible Killers Nobody Talks About

Everyone wants to talk about what makes a GCC succeed — the talent, the city, the tech stack, the executive sponsor. But after working with dozens of GCCs across India, I have noticed that the real failure points are almost never on that list. They are quieter, slower, and by the time they are visible, it is usually too late...

Year in Review · Market Analysis

2025: The Year Everyone Was Talking About GCC

If 2020-2023 was the era of GCC discovery — when global companies first seriously looked at India — then 2025 was the year of GCC conviction. Deals got bigger. Mandates got broader. CEOs started flying in to inaugurate their India centres personally. This is what I observed, what changed, and what it means for 2026...

Shammi Prabhakar Singh · Market Analysis · Read full article →
City Intelligence · GCC Location

Hyderabad vs Pune: The GCC Battle for 2025

Scale or depth? Hyderabad has been winning on volume — more new GCCs, faster growth, government support at a scale Pune cannot currently match. But Pune has something Hyderabad is still building: a 30-year engineering culture and proximity to Mumbai money. This is how I see the battle playing out...

Shammi Prabhakar Singh · City Analysis · Read full article →

Vanderlande opens India Innovation Centre in Pune. Another global leader choosing India not for cost arbitrage but for genuine innovation capability. This is the GCC story of 2025 — and it's only accelerating.

Shammi Prabhakar Singh
Founder, GCCPROs · CA

The GCC narrative has shifted fundamentally. Three years ago, companies asked "should we set up in India?" Today they ask "how fast can we scale?" That is a profound change in confidence — and it changes everything about how we should think about the next 5 years.
